questions.name: loose-equality-risk
questions.question_text: A developer writes if (userAge == '28'). What is the potential problem with this comparison?

answers.text: It uses type coercion — == converts types before comparing, which can produce unexpected matches
answers.html: It uses type coercion — == converts types before comparing, which can produce unexpected matches

answers.text: It always returns false because the types are different
answers.html: It always returns false because the types are different

answers.text: The if statement requires braces to work correctly
answers.html: The if statement requires braces to work correctly

answers.text: This will throw a ReferenceError at runtime
answers.html: This will throw a ReferenceError at runtime

questions.name: devtools-debugging
questions.question_text: A function you wrote is returning undefined instead of the expected value. Which DevTools approach would most directly help you trace the problem?

answers.text: Open the Console tab and add console.log() calls inside the function to inspect intermediate values and confirm which line produces undefined
answers.html: Open the Console tab and add console.log() calls inside the function to inspect intermediate values and confirm which line produces undefined

answers.text: Open the Network tab and check the HTTP status code
answers.html: Open the Network tab and check the HTTP status code

answers.text: Open the Elements tab and inspect the DOM structure
answers.html: Open the Elements tab and inspect the DOM structure

answers.text: Open the Application tab and clear localStorage
answers.html: Open the Application tab and clear localStorage
